expectations.text: simplify-step-present: Output shows /simplify step in the pipeline
expectations.evidence: No /simplify step appears in the pipeline. Workflow goes directly: BA -> Architect -> QA -> Developer+QA (parallel) -> Code Reviewer+Security (parallel). The /simplify skill is not invoked at any point.

expectations.text: remediation-loop-shown: Output shows a remediation loop when blocking findings occur
expectations.evidence: When Code Reviewer finds 1 Blocker (public AuditLog entity fields), an ad-hoc rework step is created: Developer fixes Blocker -> Code Reviewer re-reviews -> Approved. However, the output explicitly notes this is improvised ('Skill ไม่ได้กำหนด explicit workflow สำหรับ re-work loop').

expectations.text: remediation-max-2-cycles: Output mentions max 2 remediation cycles before escalation
expectations.evidence: No mention of max 2 remediation cycles. The output explicitly identifies this as a gap: 'No max retry limit for rework -- If Developer fix introduces a new Blocker, there is no defined limit on how many cycles to attempt -- Risk of infinite loop without explicit bounds.'
Task Classification shows 'Selected Workflow: New Feature' with reason that it involves adding a new endpoint with new domain logic, DB persistence, and audit logging.

expectations.text: context-passed-between-agents: BA output passed to Architect, Architect output passed to Developer
expectations.evidence: Architect prompt includes 'Prior context from Business Analyst' with user story, business rules, acceptance criteria, and stack info. Developer prompt includes 'Prior context from Architect' with API contract, module structure, data flow, entity design, and transaction details.

expectations.text: structured-summary: Output has Summary section with Task, Workflow, Agents Used
expectations.evidence: Summary section contains: Task (เพิ่ม endpoint สำหรับ create consent record), Workflow (New Feature), Agents Used (Business Analyst, Architect, QA, Developer, Code Reviewer, Security). Each agent has a sub-section with key outputs.
